# Spectrum Web Components (1st-gen)

> 1st-gen (@spectrum-web-components/) component library implementing the Spectrum design system.
> This index lists getting started, guides, and 1st-gen components.


## Getting started

- [Getting started](getting-started.md): Install and use Spectrum Web Components
- [Migrating to Spectrum 2](migrating-to-spectrum2.md): Migration from Spectrum 1 to 2
- [Dev mode](dev-mode.md): Development workflow


## Guides
- [Adding a component](guides/adding-component.md)
- [Configuring OpenWC](guides/configuring-openwc.md)
- [Generating components](guides/generating-components.md)
- [Styling components](guides/styling-components.md)
- [Writing changesets](guides/writing-changesets.md)

## Components
- [Accordion](components/accordion.md): The `<sp-accordion>` element contains a list of items that can be expanded or collapsed to reveal additional content ...
- [Accordion Item](components/accordion-item.md): The `<sp-accordion-item>` element represents a single item in an [`<sp-accordion>`](../accordion/) parent element. It...
- [Action Bar](components/action-bar.md): A `<sp-action-bar>` delivers a floating action bar that is a convenient way to deliver stateful actions in cases like...
- [Action Button](components/action-button.md): An `<sp-action-button>` represents an action a user can take.
- [Action Group](components/action-group.md): 1st-gen component documentation
- [Action Menu](components/action-menu.md): An `<sp-action-menu>` is an action button that triggers an overlay with `<sp-menu-items>` for activation. Use an `<sp...

- [Asset](components/asset.md): 1st-gen component documentation

- [Badge](components/badge.md): `<sp-badge>` elements display a small amount of color-categorized metadata. They're ideal for getting a user's attent...
- [Breadcrumb Item](components/breadcrumb-item.md): For use within an [`<sp-breadcrumbs>`](/components/breadcrumbs) element, an `<sp-breadcrumb-item>` represents a singl...

- [Button](components/button.md): An `<sp-button>` represents an action a user can take. sp-buttons can be clicked

- [Contextual Help](components/contextual-help.md): An `<sp-contextual-help>` shows a user extra information about the state of either an adjacent component or an entire...
- [Dialog](components/dialog.md): `sp-dialog` displays important information that users need to acknowledge. They appear over the interface and block f...

- [Divider](components/divider.md): `sp-divider` brings clarity to a layout by grouping and dividing content that exists in close proximity. It can also ...
- [Dropzone](components/dropzone.md): A `<sp-dropzone>` is an area on the screen into which an object can be dragged and dropped to accomplish a task. For ...

- [Help Text](components/help-text.md): An `<sp-help-text>` provides either an informative description or an error message that gives more context about what...
- [Help Text Mixin](components/help-text-mixin.md): 1st-gen component documentation

- [Number Field](components/number-field.md): `<sp-number-field>` elements are used for numeric inputs.

- [Popover](components/popover.md): An `<sp-popover>` is used to display transient content (menus, options, additional actions etc.) and appears when cli...
- [Progress Bar](components/progress-bar.md): An `<sp-progress-bar>` is used to visually show the progression of a system operation such as downloading, uploading,...
- [Progress Circle](components/progress-circle.md): An `<sp-progress-circle>` shows the progression of a system operation such as downloading, uploading, processing, etc...
- [Radio](components/radio.md): `<sp-radio>` and [`<sp-radio-group>`](../radio-group) allow users to select a single option from a list of mutually e...
- [Radio Group](components/radio-group.md): [`<sp-radio>`](../radio) and `<sp-radio-group>` allow users to select a single option from a list of mutually exclusi...
- [Search](components/search.md): The `<sp-search>` element is used for searching and filtering items.
- [Sidenav](components/sidenav.md): Side navigation allows users to locate information and features within the UI.

- [Slider](components/slider.md): `<sp-slider>` allows users to quickly select a value within a range. They should be used when the upper and lower bou...

- [Swatch Group](components/swatch-group.md): An `<sp-swatch-group>` group is a grouping of `<sp-swatch>` elements that are related to each other.
- [Switch](components/switch.md): An `<sp-switch>` is used to turn an option on or off. Switches allow users to select the state of a single option at ...

- [Tabs Overflow](components/tabs-overflow.md): The `<sp-tabs-overflow>` is a decorative component that works in conjunction with the `<sp-tabs>` component. It enabl...
- [Tag](components/tag.md): 1st-gen component documentation

- [Textarea](components/textarea.md): `sp-textfield[multiline]` components are text areas that allow users to input custom multiline text entries with a ke...
- [Textfield](components/textfield.md): `sp-textfield` components are text boxes that allow users to input custom text entries with a keyboard. Various decor...
- [Thumbnail](components/thumbnail.md): An `sp-thumbnail` can be used in a variety of locations as a way to display a preview of an image, layer, or effect.
- [Toast](components/toast.md): `<sp-toast>` elements display brief, temporary notifications. They are noticeable but do not disrupt the user experie...
- [Tooltip](components/tooltip.md): `<sp-tooltip>` allow users to get contextual help or information about specific components when hovering or focusing ...
- [Tooltip Directive](components/tooltip-directive.md): While `<sp-tooltip>` elements are generally fairly innocuous amounts of DOM, it is possible impact performance with t...
- [Top Nav](components/top-nav.md): `<sp-top-nav>` delivers site navigation, particularly for when that navigation will change the majority of the page's...
- [Top Nav Item](components/top-nav-item.md): `<sp-top-nav-item>` represents individual navigation links within a [`<sp-top-nav>` component](/components/top-nav). ...
- [Tray](components/tray.md): `<sp-tray>` elements are typically used to portray information on mobile device or smaller screens.
